channel.title: Matthew on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Matthew on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Matthew 1
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 1

1 The Book of the Beginning of Jesus Messiah,

Son of-David, Son of-Abraham

2Abraham, father of Isaac, father of Jacob, father of
Judah and his brothers;
3Judah was father of Peres and Zerah by Tamar;
Peres was father of Hezron, father of
Ram, 4father of Amminadab, father of Nahshon, father of
Salmon, 5father of Boaz by Rahab;
Boaz was father of Obed by Ruth;
Obed was father of
Jesse, 6father of David the king.
David was father of
Solomon by his wife, who
had been Uriah’s wife. 7Solomon was father of Rehoboam, father of
Abijah, father of Asaph, 8father of Jehoshaphat, father of
Joram, father of Uzziah, 9father of Jotham, father of
Ahaz, father of Hezekiah, 10father of Manasseh, father of
Amos, father of Josiah, 11father of Jechoniah and his brothers
at the exile to Babylon.
12After the exile,
Jechoniah was father of Shealtiel, father of Zerubbabel, 13father of
Abiud, father of Eliakim, father of Azor, 14father of
Zadok, father of Achim, father of Eliud, 15father of
Eleazar, father of Matthan, father of Jacob, 16father of Joseph
husband of Mary,
who gave birth to Jesus,
called Messiah.

17So all the generations from Abraham to David (are) fourteen generation, and from David to the carrying-off of-Babylon (are) fourteen generations, and from the carrying-off of-Babylon to [the] Messiah (are) fourteen generations.

item.title: Matthew 2
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 2

            1 And [the] Jesus having-been-born in Bethlehem of-[the] Judaea in (the) days of-Herod the king, behold, mage from (the) East arrived in Jerusalem, 2 saying, “Where is the-one having-been-born king of-the Jews? For we-saw the star of-him in the East and we-came to-worship him.” 3 And having-heard, Herod the king was-troubled and all Jerusalem with him; 4 and having-assembled all the chief-priests and scribes of-the people, he-inquired from them where the Messiah would-be-born. 5 And the/they said to-him, “In Bethlehem of-[the] Judaea, for thus it-is-written through the prophet,

item.title: Matthew 3
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 3

            1 And in [the] those days, John the Baptist comes preaching in the desert of-[the] Judaea 2 [and]ms, saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of-[the] heaven came-near.” 3 For this is the-one having-been-spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

                        “(A) voice shouting in the desert,

‘Prepare the way of-(the)-Lord;

                                    make the paths of-him straight.’”

4 And [the] John himself was-having the garment of-him from hairs of-(a)-camel and (a) leather belt around the wait of-him, and the food of-him was locusts and field/wild honey. 5 Then Jerusalem was-coming-out to-him and all [the] Judaea and all the territory of-the Jordan, 6 and they-were-being-baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing the sins of-them.

item.title: Matthew 4
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 4

1 Then [the] Jesus was-led by the Spirit into the desert to-be-tempted by the devil. 2 And having-fasted for-forty days and forty nights, he-was-hungry afterwards.

3 And the tempter, having-come, said to-him, “If you-are (the) Son of-[the] God, say [in-order]-that [the] these stones become loaves.”

4 And the/he, having-answered, said, “It-is-written,

‘[The/A] man shall┘not└live by bread alone,

                                    but by every word proceeding

item.title: Matthew 5
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 5

1 And having-seen the crowds, he-went-up one the/a mountain; and he having-sat-down, the disciples of-him came-to him. 2 And having-opened the mouth of-him, he-began-to-teach them saying,

            3 “Blessed (are) the poor in-respect-to-the spirit,

                        because of-them is the kingdom of-[the] heaven[s].

            4“Blessed (are) the-ones mourning,

                        because they will-be-comforted.

            5“Blessed (are) the humble,

item.title: Matthew 6
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 6

1 “[And]ms be-careful not to-do the righteousness of-you in-front-of [the] men/people unto [the] being-seen by them. And if not, you-do┘not└have (a) reward from the Father of-you in [the] heaven[s].

2 “So when you-do alms, do┘not└blow (a) trumpet in-front-of you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets in-order-that they-may-be-glorified by [the] men. Truly I-say to-you, they-have the reward of-them. 3 But you doing alms, let┘the left (hand) of-you not└know what the right (hand) of-you is-doing, 4 in-order-that the alms of-you may-be in [the] secret, and the Father of-you the-One seeing in [the] secret will-reward you.

item.title: Matthew 7
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 7

1 “Do┘not└criticize in-order-that you-may┘not└be-criticized. 2 For with what criticism you-criticize you-will-be-criticized, and with what measure you-measure it-will-be-measured to-you. 3 And why do┘you└look-at the piece-of-sawdust in the eye of-the brother of-you, and you-do┘not└recognize the log in [the] your eye? 4 Or how will-you-say to-the brother of-you, ‘Let me-take-out the piece-of-sawdust from the eye of-you,’ and behold the/a log (is) in the eye of-you? 5 Hypocrite! First take-out the log in the eye of-you, and then you-will-see-clearly to-take-out the piece-of-sawdust from the eye of-the brother of-you.

item.title: Matthew 8
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 8

1 And he having-come-down from the mountain, many crowds followed him. 2 And behold (a) leper, coming-to (him), knelt to-him, saying, “Lord, if you-are-willing, you-are-able to-cleanse me.”

3 And having-stretched-out the/his hand, he-touched him, saying, “I-am-willing; be-cleansed.” And immediately the leprosy of-him was-cleansed.

4 And [the] Jesus says to-him, “See (that) you-say nothing to nobody/anybody, but go show yourself to-the/a priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded for (a) testimony to-them.”

item.title: Matthew 9
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 9

            1 And having-gotten into the boat, he-crossed-over and came into [the] his-own city. 2 And behold, they-were-bringing to-him (a) paralytic laid on (a) stretcher. And [the] Jesus, having-seen the faith of-them, said to-the paralytic, “Be-encouraged, son; the sins of-you are-forgiven.”

3 And behold some of-the scribes said to each other, “This-one is-blaspheming.”

4 And [the] Jesus, having-seen the thoughts of-them, said, “Why are-you thinking bad-things in the hearts of-you? 5 For what is easier, to-say, ‘The sins of-you are-forgiven,’ or to-say, ‘Get-up and walk’? 6 But in-order-that you-may-know that the Son of-[the] Man has authority on [the] earth to-forgive sins”—then he-says to-the paralytic, “Get-up, pick-up the stretcher of-you and go into the house of-you.” 7 And having-arisen he-went-away into the house of-him. 8 And the crowd[s], having-seen, were-afraid and glorified [the] God the-one having-given such authority to-[the] men.

item.title: Matthew 10
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 10

            1 And having-summoned the twelve disciples of-him, he-gave them authority of-unclean spirits so-as to-cast┘them└out and to-heal every disease and every sickness. 2 And these are the names of-the twelve apostles: first Simon, the-one called Peter; and Andrew, the brother of-him; and James, the (son) of-[the] Zebedee, and John, the brother of-him; 3 Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas and Matthew, the tax-collector; James the (son) of-[the] Alpheus, and Thaddeus, 4 Simon the Cananaean, and Judas [the] Iscariot, the-one also having-betrayed him.

item.title: Matthew 11
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 11

            1 And it-happened when [the] Jesus finished giving-directions to-the twelve disciples of-him, (that) he-left from-there [the] to-teach and preach in the towns of-them.

2 And [the] John, having-heard in [the] prison the works of-[the] Messiah, having-sent through the disciples of-him, 3 he-said to-him, “Are you the-One coming, or are-we-waiting-for another?”

 4 And [the] Jesus, having-answered, said to-them, “Having-gone, report to-John what-things you-hear and see. 5 (The) blind see and (the) lame walk, lepers are-cleansed and (the) deaf hear, and (the) dead are-raised and (the) poor are-evangelized. 6 And blessed is whoever2 is┘not└offended/disappointed in me.”

item.title: Matthew 12
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 12

            1 In that [the] time [the] Jesus went through the grain-fields on-the Sabbath[s]. and the disciples of-him were-hungry and they-began to-pluck grain-heads and to-eat (the). 2 And the Pharisees, having seen, said to-him, “Behold, the disciples of-you are-doing what is┘not└lawful to-do on (the) Sabbath.”

3 And the/he said to-them,

“Did-you┘not└read what David did when he-was-hungry and the-ones with him? 4 How he-entered into the house of-[the] God and ate the loaves of-[the] presentation, which was not lawful for-him to-eat nor for-the-ones with him except for-the priests only? 5 Or, did-you┘not└read in the Law that on-[the[] Sabbath[s] the priests in the temple defile the Sabbath and are innocent? 6 And I-say to-you that something-greater than-the temple is here. 7 And if you had-known what it-is, ‘I-desire mercy and not sacrifice,’ you-wouldhave-condemned2 the innocent 8 for the Son of-[the] Man is Lord of-the Sabbath.”

item.title: Matthew 13
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 13

            1 In [the] that day having-come-out of-the house, [the] Jesus sat-down beside the sea. 2 And many crowd[s] were-gathered-together to him, so-that he, having-gotten in (a) boat, sat-down, and all the crowd was-standing on the shore. 3 And he-said to-them many-things in parables, saying,

“Behold, the/a sowing-one went-out [the] to-sow. 4 And in [the] his sowing some fell beside the road, and the birds, having-come, devoured them. 5 And others fell on [the] rocky-ground where it-did┘not└have much soil, and immediately it-sprang on-account-of [the] not having depth of-soil. 6 And (the) sun having-risen-high, it-was-scorched and on-account-of [the] not having root it-dried-up. 7 And others fell to [the] thorns, and the thorns grew-up and choked┘it└out. 8 But others fell on [the] good [the] ground and were-bearing fruit, some (a) hundred, some sixty, and some thirty. 9 The-one having ears, let┘him└hear.”

item.title: Matthew 14
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 14

            1 At that [the] time Herod the tetrarch heard the news of-Jesus, 2 and he-said to-the servants of-him, “This is John the Baptist. He arose from the dead and on-account-of this, [the] powers are-at-work in him.” 3 For [the] Herod, having-arrested [the] John, bound [him]ms, and put (him) in prison on-account-of Herodias, the wife of-Philip, the brother of-him. 4 For [the] John kept-telling him, “It-is┘not└lawful for-you to-have her.” 5 And desiring to-put┘him└to-death, he-feared the crowd, because they-were-having/hold him as (a) prophet.

item.title: Matthew 15
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 15

1 The Pharisees and scribes from Jerusalem came-to [the] Jesus, saying, 2 “On-account-of what do┘the disciples of-you└transgress the tradition of-the elders? For they-do┘not└wash the hands [of-them]ms when they-eat bread?”

3 And the/he, having-answered, said to-them,

“On-account-of what do┘you└transgress the commandment of-[the] God on-account-of the traditions of-you? 4 For [the] God said, ‘Honor the/your father and the/your mother,’ and ‘The-one cursing father or mother—let-him-die death/surely die.’ 5 But you say, ‘Whoever2 says to-the/his father or the/his mother, “(It-is) (a) gift, whatever2 you-would-have benefited from me,’” 6 he-will┘certainly not└be-honoring the father of-him. And you-set-aside the word of-[the] God on-account-of the tradition of-you. 7 Hypocrites! Well did┘Isaiah└prophesy about you, saying,

item.title: Matthew 16
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 16

1 And the Pharisees and Sadducees, having-come-to (him), testing (him), asked him to-show them (a) sign from [the] heaven/the sky. 2 And the/he, having-answered, said to-them, [“Evening having-come, you-say, ‘Good-weather, for the sky is-bright-red.’ 3 And in-the- morning, ‘Today, bad-weather; for the sky is-red and is-dark.’ You-know to-interpret/read the face of-the sky, but are-you┘not└able (to-interpret/read) the times?] 4 An evil and adulterous generation keeps-looking-for (a) sign, and (a) sign will be-given to-it except2 the sign of-Jonah.” And having-left them, he-went-away.

item.title: Matthew 17
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 17

            1 And after six days [the] Jesus talks┘[the] Peter and James and John the brother of-him└aside, and takes┘them└up into (a) high mountain privately2. 2 And he-was-transfigured in-front-of them; and the face of-him shined like the sun, and the clothes of-him became white like [the] light. 3 And behold Moses and Elijah appeared to-them talking-with them.

4 And [the] Peter, having-answered, said to-[the] Jesus, “Lord, it-is-good for-us to-be here. If you-want, I-will-set-up three tents here, for-you one, and for-Moses one, and for-Elijah one.”

item.title: Matthew 18
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 18

1 In that [the] hour the disciples came-to [the] Jesus, saying, “Who is greater/greatest in the kingdom of-[the] heaven[s]?”

2 And having-called (a) little-child, stood him in (the) midst of-them, 3 and said,

“Truly I-say to-you, if you-do┘not└turn and become like [the] little-children, you-will┘certainly not└enter into the kingdom of-[the] heaven[s]. 4 So whoever humbles himself like [the] this little-child—this is the greater/greatest in the kingdom of-[the] heaven, 5 and whoever receives one such little-child in (the) name of-me, receives me.

item.title: Matthew 19
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 19

1 And it-happened when [the] Jesus finished [the] these words, he-departed from [the] Galilee and he-came into the mountains of-[the] Judaea beyond the Jordan. 2 And many crowd[s] followed him and he-healed them there.

3 And Pharisees came-to him, trying him and saying [whether], “Is-it lawful for-(a)-man to-divorce the wife of-him for every reason?”

4 And the/he, having-answered, said, “Did-you┘not└read that the-One having-created (them), from (the) beginning ‘made them male and female’? 5 and said, ‘For-the-sake-of this (a) man will-leave the/his father and the/his mother and unite-with the wife of-him, and the two will-become [for] one flesh; 6 so-that they-are no-longer two but one flesh. What then [the] God joined-together let┘not man└separate.”

item.title: Matthew 20
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 20

20:1“For the kingdom of-[the] heaven[s] is like (a) landowner man who went-out early to-hire workers for the vineyard of-him. 2 And having-agreed with the workers of (a) denarius for-the day, he-sent them into the vineyard of-him. 3 And having-gone-out about (the) third hour, he-saw others standing idle in the market-place, 4 and said to-them, ‘You also go into the/my vineyard, and whatever is right I-will-give you.’ 5 And the/they went-away. [And]ms again having-gone-out (the) sixth and ninth hours, he-did likewise. 6 And about the eleventh hour, having-gone-out, he-found others standing and said to-them, ‘Why are-you-standing here the whole day idle?’ 7 They-say to-him, ‘Because nobody hired us.’ He-says to-them, ‘You also go into the/my vineyard.’ 8 And evening having-come, the lord of-the vineyard says to-the foreman of-him, ‘Call the workers and give them the/their pay having-begun from the last to the first.’ 9 And having-come, the-ones about the eleventh hour received (a) denarius apiece. 10 And having-come, the first thought that they would receive more, and they received [the/a]ms denarius also. 11 And having-received (it), they-began-to-grumble against the landowner, 12 saying ‘These [the] last do for-one hour, and you-made them equal to-us the-ones having-borne the burden of-the day and the heat.’ 13 But the/he, having-answered to-one of-them, said, ‘Friend, I-did┘not└mistreat you. Did-you┘not└agree-with me of-(a)-denarius? 14 Take (the) your-thing and go. But I-want to-pay this [the] last-one as also (I-paid) you. 15 [Or]ms, is-it┘not└lawful for-me to-do what I-want with [the] my-things?’ 16 Thus the last will-be first, and the first (will be) last.”

item.title: Matthew 21
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 21

1 And when they-got-near to Jerusalem and they-came to Bethany to the Mount of-[the] Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples, 2 saying to-them, “Go into the village across-from you, and immediately you-will-find (a) donkey tied and (a) colt with her. Having-untied (them), bring (them) to-me. 3 And if anybody says anything to-you, you-shall-say [that], ‘The Lord has need of-them.’ And immediately he-will-send them.” 4 And this-thing has-happened in-order-that the-thing spoken through the prophet might-be-fulfilled, saying,

item.title: Matthew 22
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 22

            1 And having-responded, [the] Jesus spoke again in parables to-them, saying,

2“[The kingdom of-[the] heaven[s] was/is like (a) king man who made(a) marriage-feast for-the son of-him. 3 And he-sent the servants of-him to-call the-ones invited to the wedding-feast[s], and they-did┘not└want to-come. 4 Again he-sent other servants, saying, ‘Say to-the ones invited, “Behold, the meal of-me I-have prepared, the oxen of-me and the grain-fed-calves 9are) butchered. All-things (are) ready. Come to the feast[s].”’ 5 But the/they, not-paying-any-attention, went-away, one to [the] his field, and one to the business of-him. 6 And the rest, having-grabbed the servants of-him, treated-(them)-disgracefully and killed (them). 7 And the king was-furious and, having-sent the soldiers of-him, destroyed [the] those murderers and burned the city of-them. 8 Then he-tells the servants of-him, ‘The wedding-feast is ready, but the-ones invited were not worthy. 9 So go into the main-roads of-the roads and as-many-as you-find invite to the marriage-feast[s].’ 10 And having-gone-out into the roads, [the] those servants assembled all whom they-found, both evil and good, and the wedding-feast was-filled of-reclining-ones-at-table. 11 And the king, having-come-in to-inspect the-ones reclining-at-table, saw there (a) man not dressed in-dress of-(a)-wedding-feast, 12 and he-says to-him, ‘Friend, how did-you-come-in here not having dress of-(a)-wedding-feast?’ And the/he was-speechless. 13 Then the king said to-the servers, ‘Having-bound him feet and hands, throw┘him└out into the outer [the] darkness. [The] crying and [the] gnashing of-[the] teeth will-be there. 14 For many are called, but few (are) chosen.’”

item.title: Matthew 23
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 23

1 Then [the] Jesus said to-the crowd[s] and to-the disciples of-him, 2 saying,

“The scribes and the Pharisees sit on the seat of-[the] Moses. 3 So do and keep all-things as-many-as2 they-say to-you, but do┘not└do according-to the works of-them, for they-say and do┘not└do. 4 And they strap heavy loads and difficult-to-carry and put (them) on the shoulders of-[the] men, but they do┘not└want to-move them with the/a finger of-them. 5 And all the works of-them they-do unto [the] being-seen by-[the] men. For they-broaden the phylacteries of-them and lengthen the/their tassels. 6 And they-love the main-seat at [the] dinners and prominent seats in [the] synagogues 7 and [the] greetings in [the] market-places, and (love) to-be-called by [the] men ‘Rabbi.’ 8 But do┘not└you be-called ‘Rabbi.’ For One is the teacher of-you, and you are all brothers. 9 And do┘not└call ‘father’ of-you on [the] earth, for One is the heavenly [the] Father of-you. 10 Neither be-called leaders because one is leader of-you—the Messiah. 11 But the greater/greatest of-you will-be (a) minister of-you. 12 But whoever will-exalt himself will-be-humbled and who humbles himself will-be-exalted.

item.title: Matthew 24
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 24

1 And having-gone-out, [the] Jesus was-going from the temple, and the disciples of-him came to-show him the buildings of-the temple-complex. 2 And the/he, having-answered, said to-them, “Do-you┘not└see all these-things? Truly I-tell you, stone on stone will be-left that will┘certainly not└be-utterly-destroyed.”

3 And he sitting on the Mount of-[the] Olives, the disciples came-to him privately2, saying, “Tell us, when will┘these-things└be and what (will be) the sign of-[the] your coming and (the) end of-the age/world?”

item.title: Matthew 25
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 25

                        1 “Then the kingdom of-[the] heaven[s] will-be-like then virgins, who, having-taken the lamps of-them, they-went-out to meeting the groom. 2 Now five of them were foolish and five (were) wise. 3 For the foolish, having-taken the lamps of-them, did┘not└take oil with them. 4 But the wise took oil in [the] flasks with the lamps of-them. 5 And the groom delaying, they-got-drowsy and fell-asleep. 6 And during (the) middle of-(the)-night, (a) shout came,, ‘Behold, the groom! Come-out for meeting [him]ms.’ 7 Then all [the] those virgins got-up and trimmed the lamps of-them. 8 Now the foolish said to-the wise, ‘Give us of the oil of-you, because the lamps of-us are-going-out.’ 9 But the wise answered, saying, ‘No, there-will┘certainly not└be-enough for-us and for-you. Go instead to the selling-ones and buy for-yourselves.’ 10 And they going-away to-buy, the groom came, and the-ones prepared entered with him into the wedding-feast and the door was-shut. 11 And later the remaining virgins also come, saying, ‘Lord, lord, open for-us.’ 12 And the/he, having-answered, said, “Truly I-say to-you, I-do┘not└know you.’ 13 So watch, because you-do┘not└know the day nor the hour.

item.title: Matthew 26
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 26

            1 And it-happened when [the] Jesus finished all [the] these words (that) he-said to-the disciples of-him. 2“You-know that after two days comes the Passover, and the Son of-[the] Man is delivered unto [the] being-crucified.” 3 Then the chief-priests and the elders of-the people gathered-together in the court of-the high-priest the-one called Caiaphas, 4 and they-plotted in-order-that they-might-arrest [the] Jesus by-stealth and put┘(him)└to-death. 5 And they-were-saying, “Not in the feast, in-order-that (a) riot not arise among the people.”

item.title: Matthew 27
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 27

            1 And morning having-come, all the chief-priests and the elders of-the people took counsel against [the] Jesus so to-put┘him└to death; 2 and having-bound him, they-led┘(him)└away and delivered (him) to Pilate the governor.

            3 The Judas the traitor, having-seen that he-was-condemned, having-regretted, returned the thirty [the] silver[s]-shekels to-the chief-priests and elders, 4 saying, “I-sinned, having-betrayed innocent blood.”

item.title: Matthew 28
item.description:
Matthew Chapter 28

            1 After the Sabbath[s], at-the dawning toward (day) one of the Sabbath[s]. Mary the Magdalene and the other Mary came to-see the grave. 2 And behold (a) great earthquake happened, for (an) angel of (the) Lord, having-descended from heaven and having-come-to (it), rolled┘the stone└away and sat-down on it. 3 And the sight of-him was like lightning and the garment of-him (was) white like snow. 4 And from the fear of-him, the-ones keeping watch shook and became like dead-(men). 5 And the angel, having-answered said to the women, “Do┘not you└be-afraid, for I-know that you-are-seeking Jesus the-One crucified. 6 He-is not here, for he-arose even-as he-said. Come, see the place where he-was-lying. 7 And quickly having-gone, say to-the disciples of-him [that], ‘He-arose from the dead-(ones), and behold, he-goes-ahead-of you into the Galilee. You-will-see him there.’ Behold, I-told you.”
